rulings.Case Title: EXPENSIFY, INC., A DELAWARE CORPORATION VS. TRILLER, INC., A DELAWARE CORPORATION ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion Calendar for Friday, January 10, 2025, Line 1, PETITIONER EXPENSIFY, INC., A DELAWARE CORPORATION'S MOTION TO CONFIRM ARBITRATION AWARD. Petitioner Expensify, Inc.'s petition to confirm arbitration is GRANTED. (The Court's complete tentative ruling has been emailed to the parties.) For the 9:30 a.m. Law & Motion calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(301/JMQ)

rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Stay Proceedings On Injunctive Relief And To Reschedule The Hearing For The Writ Of Mandamus
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion Calendar for Friday, January 10, 2025, Line 2, PETITIONER KLAUS BIESENBACH's Motion To Stay Proceedings On Injunctive Relief And To Reschedule The Hearing For The Writ Of Mandamus. Petitioner Klaus Biesenbach's motion to stay proceedings on injunctive relief and to reschedule hearing for the writ of mandate is DENIED. On November 11, 2025, Biesenbach filed a "petition for injunctive relief and mandatory investigation" against the City and County of San Francisco and its agencies. Biesenbach requested this court issue a writ and grant an injunction to investigate an alleged pattern of serious technological abuses against him in violation of Welfare & Institutions Code section 15630. On December 6, 2024, Biesenbach filed a "Notice of Hearing for Petition for Injunctive Relief, Writ of Mandate, In Camera Review, and Court-Monitored Compliance," noticing the hearing for January 17, 2025. On December 18, 2024, the City filed a Demurrer to Petition for Injunctive Relief and Mandatory Investigation," noticing a hearing for January 17, 2025. Biesenbach now seeks to stay proceedings including his request for injunctive relief, so the court can first resolve the writ and its legal questions of "whether Respondents acted in accordance with their legal obligations." Biesenbach cites CCP 128(a)(3) as the basis for his stay request. The court has reviewed the register of actions. Biesenbach has not shown that proceeding with the motion for the writ, though apparently on a latter day, and staying the injunctive claim(s) over the City's objection would not promote justice or judicial economy. For the time being, matters will stay on calendar as currently set. For the 9:30 a.m. Law & Motion calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(301/JMQ)
